title: Describing Actions
learningGoal: The learner will write 3 short sentences constructing active statements using ~masu verbs and the wo, ni, and de particles.
instructions: Write three short sentences in Japanese. Follow the prompts carefully to construct the sentences with the correct particles. Remember, the verb must go at the VERY END of the sentence.

contentItems.text: The particle 'wo' connects an object to a verb. The particle 'de' connects a location to a verb. The particle 'ni' connects a time/destination to a verb.

writingPrompt: Translate these three sentences: 1) I eat a meal at the cafe. 2) I go to school at 9 o'clock. 3) I listen to music at home.
promptGoal: To construct SOV sentences demonstrating proficiency with wo, ni, and de.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: カフェで ごはんを たべます。くじに がっこうに いきます。うちで おんがくを ききます。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Kafe de gohan wo tabemasu. Ku-ji ni gakkou ni ikimasu. Uchi de ongaku wo kikimasu.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: I eat a meal at the cafe. I go to school at 9 o'clock. I listen to music at home.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect particle usage and word order. Also correctly used the exception 'ku-ji' for 9 o'clock.

evaluationCriteria.description: Are all three verbs (tabemasu, ikimasu, kikimasu) at the end of their respective sentences?
Did the learner use 'de' for the cafe/home and 'ni' for school/9 o'clock?
Did the learner use 'wo' after meal (gohan) and music (ongaku)?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: カフェで ごはんを たべます。くじに がっこうに いきます。うちで おんがくを ききます。
acceptableAlternates: わたしは カフェで ごはんを たべます。わたしは くじに がっこうに いきます。わたしは うちで おんがくを ききます。
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: SOV sentence structure with wo, ni, de.

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic writing! You have successfully mastered basic Japanese sentence structure.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review the particles. 'de' is for where an action happens (Cafe de). 'wo' connects the object to the verb (Gohan wo tabemasu).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you didn't accidentally write 'kyuu-ji' instead of 'ku-ji' for 9 o'clock.

hintText: To type the particle 'wo', type 'w' then 'o'. It will become を.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Kafe ni tabemasu'. You eat AT a cafe, so it must be 'Kafe de'.
